title: The Sign of Jonah
subtitle: The Pharisees demand a sign from heaven, and the Lord gives them only the sign of His own death and burial.

stillness: Be still, and do not search above your strength.
Lay down your curiosity, and your appetite for signs.
Here is the one sign you need: the death and burial of the Lord.
gospel: Master, we wish to see a sign from thee.
And He, answering, said to them:
A wicked and adulterous generation seeketh a sign,
and a sign shall not be given it,
but the sign of Jonah the prophet.
For as Jonah was in the whale's belly three days and three nights,
so shall the Son of man be in the heart of the earth three days and three nights.
The men of Nineveh shall rise in judgment with this generation,
behold: See how, when the Jews reviled Christ, saying He cast out devils by the prince of devils, He answered them gently, disproving their words by reasons;
but here, where they flatter Him with praise, calling Him Master, He touches them harshly.
For He shows Himself superior to both passions:
neither is He drawn into anger by insults, nor softened by flattery.
Their words are full of both flattery and mockery: now they call Him a demoniac, now they call Him Master, both with deadly intent.
They demand a sign from heaven, not from devotion, that they might believe, but from curiosity, that they might contradict Him the more easily.
See, then: He does not give them the sign of His divinity from heaven, which they sought, for by their curiosity and malice they were unworthy to see it.
He gives instead the sign of Jonah: a sign of humility and lowliness, against their pride.
consider: When they had seen many signs, as though they had seen none, so did they seek a sign.
Truly they saw nothing, for they saw with the bodily eye, but not with spiritual affection.
They thought the miracles of Christ small, because they were wrought, for the most part, about lower things, the healing of bodies and the like.
So they sought signs about the heavenly bodies, as in the days of Moses, of Samuel, of Elijah, of Joshua.
But he who slanders what he sees with his eyes and holds in his hand, what will he do with things that come from heaven?
You, wicked generation, ask for a heavenly sign, a sign of power and glory from on high, and it shall not be given you,
but only an earthly sign, a sign of passion and of weakness, signified in the sign and deed of Jonah the prophet.
Three things were in Jonah: the swallowing, the holding, the casting forth.
The swallowing was a sign of the death of Christ; the holding, of His burial; the casting forth, of His resurrection.
The death and Passion of Christ is the chief and final sign, the sign of signs, into which all signs lead.
By those who sought a sign from heaven are signified the curious and the proud, who pry into heavenly things above their strength,
who search the divine too curiously, having left the way, the precepts and the counsels of the Lord, by which one comes to those things.
And therefore they are crushed by glory, and slip sometimes into erroneous teachings, sometimes into sins of spirit and flesh.
To such it is said: a wicked and adulterous generation, for they seek what cannot be known, nor is profitable to faith or to morals;
and so the devil deceives them and swallows them down, as the whale swallowed Jonah.
A wise man is struck in the face when he is praised to his face.
Better it is to be corrected by the wise, than to be deceived by the flattery of fools.
prayer: Lord Jesus Christ, good Master, work in me the sign of Thy grace unto good,
that me, a wretched man, whom that great whale and dragon has swallowed up by sins spiritual and carnal, as by days and nights, and these three, by consent, by deed, and by habit,
Thou wouldst mercifully deign to deliver from his jaws.
Make me also to bewail my past sins, and to beware of those to come.
Dry me from the moisture of concupiscence, that it may find no rest in me;
cleanse me with the brooms of confession from the filth of my vices, and adorn me with true virtues:
lest he find me empty of good things, and make me to sin again. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Vere enim nullum viderunt, quia corporali aspectu viderunt, non spirituali affectu.
latinJewel.english: For truly they saw nothing, because they saw with the bodily eye, but not with spiritual affection.
